questions.question: What is the topic for Week 5?
options: C. THE WORLD AND ITS LUSTS
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly given for Lesson Five is MY ENEMY: “THE FLESH”.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 4?
questions.explanation: Hodegeo Pneuma was the topic for Week 4.

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is described as our 'external' enemy?
questions.explanation: The introduction states: 'The world' is our external enemy.

questions.question: Our enemy, the Devil, has a ________ inside of us that cooperates with him in his battle against us.
questions.explanation: The text says 'the Devil, has “a stronghold” inside of us'.

questions.question: The one controlled by the flesh is referred to as a spiritual man.
questions.explanation: The text states 'The one controlled by the flesh is also referred to as a natural or carnal man.'

questions.question: So then, those who are in the flesh cannot ________ God.
questions.explanation: Romans 8:8 states 'those who are in the flesh cannot please God.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Romans 8:8.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 5 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: So then, those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Romans 8:8.

questions.question: What is the ultimate consequence of being 'in the flesh' according to the memory verse?
options: C. You will be cast out of the church
questions.explanation: The verse says 'those who are in the flesh cannot please God.'

questions.question: What is the exact heading of Outline I?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE “FLESH” DESCRIBED'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, 'the flesh' is literally our physical body.
questions.explanation: Outline I clearly states: '“The flesh” is not our body, but the sinful nature that dwells in our body.'

questions.question: According to Romans 7:5 and Ephesians 2:2-3 cited in the lesson, what was on the throne of our hearts before we were saved?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'Before we were saved, ‘self’ was on the throne of our hearts (Romans 7:5; Ephesians 2:2-3).'

questions.question: What happened to 'self' when we received Christ and He came to live in us (Galatians 2:20)?
options: B. It submitted fully to Christ instantly
C. It did not move out and wants to continue its rule
questions.explanation: The text says 'However, ‘self’ did not move out. Not only is it still there, but it wants to continue its rule in our hearts'.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the three things the Bible tells us about 'self' according to Outline I?
options: B. Sin comes from our sinful nature (Mark 7:21-23)
C. 'Self' can be reformed into the Spirit (John 3:6)
D. The flesh is Satan’s 'stronghold' for sinning (Galatians 5:19-21)
questions.explanation: Reforming self is not listed. The three things are: never obey God, sin comes from it, and it is Satan's stronghold.

questions.question: Which form of 'self' expects people to look up to and honour him, declaring himself as first and above all?
questions.explanation: The text defines Self-pride as 'Declares himself as first and above all. He expects people to look up to and honour him'.

questions.question: Which biblical reference is used to illustrate 'Self-love' (affirming that the person who should be loved more than anyone else is himself)?
questions.explanation: The text uses '(Luke 12:16-20)' for Self-love.

questions.question: Which form of 'self' is described as believing that he is poor and not treated right, always complaining and never satisfied?
questions.explanation: The lesson defines this as Self-pity.

questions.question: Which Old Testament passage is used to illustrate 'Self-righteousness'?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(1 Kings 19:14)' for Self-righteousness.

questions.question: How is 'Self-will' described in the lesson?
options: A. Always wants to have his way and does not care whether it contradicts God’s will.
B. Satisfied with his own goodness and morality.
C. Believes he is poor and not treated right.
D. Expects people to look up to and honour him.
questions.explanation: The lesson states: 'Always wants to have his way. He will do what he wants to do and does not care whether it contradicts God’s will.'

questions.question: What is the exact heading of Outline II?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'VICTORY THROUGH GOD'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, a sinner can easily come into the presence of God even with the old sinful life still active.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Since no sinner can come into the presence of God, our old sinful life must come to an end...'

questions.question: According to Romans 6:6-7, what was the first wonderful thing God did for us out of His love?
options: B. He ended our old self-life and created something entirely new that sin cannot touch
C. He gave us wealth and power
D. He made us rulers over demons
questions.explanation: The text lists: '1. He ended our old self-life and created something entirely new i.e. something that sin cannot touch (Romans 6:6-7).'

questions.question: When Christ rose from the dead, we rose with Him as 'new persons' in God’s new ________ (2 Corinthians 5:17).
questions.explanation: The text states 'new persons” in God’s new creation (2 Corinthians 5:17)'.

questions.question: Which verse is cited for the truth that God gave Christ to us as our new Master, and in Him we live to do the will of His father?
questions.explanation: The text lists '3. He gave Christ to us as our new Master... John 3:30.'

questions.question: The lesson asserts that as long as we are in our bodies, we can completely get rid of 'self'.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says: 'As long as we are in our bodies, we can never get rid of self, but we can avoid being ruled by self.'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the steps given in Outline II to subdue self?
options: A. Choose to keep self on the Cross
C. Live the Christ-life by bearing the fruit of the Spirit
D. Perform daily self-flagellation to weaken the flesh
questions.explanation: Self-flagellation is not mentioned. The three steps are keeping self on the cross, making Christ king, and bearing fruit of the Spirit.

questions.question: Which verse is cited for the step: 'Choose to keep self on the Cross'?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Choose to keep self on the Cross (Galatians 5:24)'.

questions.question: Be in control of your flesh with the help of the ________ (Galatians 5:16).
questions.explanation: The conclusion reads 'Be in control of your flesh with the help of the Holy Spirit'.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh) nothing good dwells; for to will is present with me...'
questions.verse_text: For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh) nothing good dwells; for to will is present with me...
questions.explanation: This is verse 18 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Romans 7:19), what struggle does the writer express?
options: A. He wants to preach but lacks the words
B. He does the evil he wills not to do, instead of the good he wills to do
C. He faces physical persecution from the Jews
D. He cannot understand the mysteries of God
questions.explanation: Romans 7:19 says 'For the good that I will to do, I do not do; but the evil I will not to do, that I practice.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Now if I do what I will not to do, it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.'
questions.verse_text: Now if I do what I will not to do, it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.
questions.explanation: This is verse 20 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Romans 7:22), in what does the writer delight according to the inward man?
options: C. The traditions of the elders
D. The fellowship of the brethren
questions.explanation: Romans 7:22 says 'For I delight in the law of God according to the inward man.'

questions.question: The introduction states that the Devil has a stronghold inside of us. Which biblical reference is given to support the Devil's cooperation in his battle against us?
questions.explanation: The text lists '(Matthew 13:25, 39a)' immediately after mentioning the Devil's battle against us and his stronghold.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that 'self' is Satan's stronghold for sinning?
questions.explanation: The text says 'the flesh is Satan’s “stronghold” for sinning (Galatians 5:19-21).'

questions.question: To live the Christ-life, Outline II says we must bear what?
questions.explanation: The outline says 'live the Christ-life by bearing the fruit of the Spirit (Galatians 5:22-23).'
